brief: Tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate, with the US threatening severe military action against Iran and the Houthis in Yemen threatening to blockade the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a vital trade route. These developments have significant implications for global economic stability, with rising oil prices and disrupted shipping routes already affecting Indian airlines and the country's economy. Meanwhile, the Reserve Bank of India has warned that the Iran war and an uneven monsoon are two potential risks that could impact India's economic growth, inflation, and policy outlook, highlighting the need for careful monitoring of global and domestic factors.

Geopolitics.title: U.N. Demands Probe into War Crime Allegations in Mali
Geopolitics.summary: The UN human rights office has called for an investigation into allegations of war crimes in Mali, including torture and execution of surrendering soldiers by insurgents and separatists. These acts could constitute war crimes under international law. The investigation aims to hold perpetrators accountable and ensure justice for victims.

Geopolitics.title: New threat by Yemen's Houthis could widen Iran war, put another trade chokepoint at risk
Geopolitics.summary: Houthi rebels have threatened to blockade the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, a vital trade route that connects the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden. This action could significantly impact international shipping and oil supplies, escalating tensions and risking a wider regional conflict. The strait is a critical chokepoint for global trade.

Geopolitics.title: Pakistan rejects Amnesty call for war crimes probe into Kabul airstrike
Geopolitics.summary: Pakistan has rejected Amnesty International's call for an investigation into a recent airstrike on a drug rehabilitation center in Kabul, which the rights group alleges could be a war crime. Pakistan claims the operation was lawful self-defense against terrorists, but Amnesty found no evidence to support this claim. The incident has escalated cross-border hostilities between Pakistan and the affected nation.

Geopolitics.title: Escalating Tensions: Trump Threatens Iran Amidst Rising Oil Prices
Geopolitics.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened severe military action against Iran following attacks on Saudi oil tankers, intensifying the Middle Eastern conflict. The situation has led to rising oil prices, inflation, and disrupted global shipping routes, with further consequences for diplomatic relations and international trade. The conflict has significant implications for global economic stability.

Geopolitics.title: 26,000 flights cancelled, longer routes and higher fuel bills: How a war far away has hit Indian flyers
Geopolitics.summary: The West Asia crisis has affected Indian airlines, with thousands of international flights cancelled due to airspace restrictions. Carriers are facing rising fuel costs, longer journeys, and schedule disruptions, impacting Indian flyers and the aviation industry as a whole. The crisis has significant implications for India's air travel and trade.

Economy.title: RBI Says India's Economy Is Strong; But Two Risks Could Change Everything
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has stated that India's economy remains strong, but two potential risks, the Iran war and an uneven monsoon, could impact growth, inflation, and policy outlook. These factors could pose significant challenges to the country's economic stability. The RBI's warning highlights the need for careful monitoring of global and domestic factors.

Economy.title: Satcom set to play a transformative role; opportunities ahead vast: Trai Chairman
Economy.summary: According to the Trai Chairman, satellite communications are expected to play a significant role in expanding connectivity across India, supporting strategic areas, and bridging the digital divide. The space economy is projected to grow substantially by 2030, driven by private participation and innovation. This growth is expected to drive technological advancement and inclusive growth.

Economy.title: Foreign banks show strongest rate transmission in current RBI easing cycle: Bulletin
Economy.summary: Foreign banks have shown the strongest transmission of policy easing during the current rate-cut cycle, with sharper reductions in lending and deposit rates. The Reserve Bank of India reduced its repo rate by 1.25 percentage points between February 2025 and May 2026. This easing cycle has had a significant impact on the banking sector.

Economy.title: RBI sold $6 billion in May to defend oil-hit Indian rupee, data shows
Economy.summary: The Reserve Bank of India sold $6.1 billion in foreign exchange during May to defend the Indian rupee, which was impacted by surging oil prices. The central bank also implemented policy measures to encourage dollar inflows into India. This intervention aimed to stabilize the currency and mitigate the effects of oil price volatility.

Economy.title: India must cut launch costs, boost local manufacturing to become world's third-largest space economy: DoT official
Economy.summary: To become the world's third-largest space economy by 2033, India needs to reduce launch costs and increase local manufacturing of materials. The government also aims to emphasize satellite applications in the downstream sector to enhance revenue. Strengthening regulatory functions will be crucial to achieving this goal.

Economy.title: Commerce Secretary highlights resilient economy, record exports at WTO Trade Policy Review
Economy.summary: The Commerce Secretary has highlighted India's resilient economy and record exports at the WTO Trade Policy Review. India has reaffirmed its support for a balanced, rules-based, and development-oriented World Trade Organisation. The country's strong economic performance and export growth are significant achievements.

Business.summary: Corporate India has invested Rs. 17,377 crore in environmental sustainability over the past decade, but the funding remains concentrated among a few companies and is not always aligned with the country's greatest environmental challenges. This move aims to address the court's workload and improve its efficiency. The expansion of the court's strength is significant as it may impact the judiciary's ability to handle cases and deliver justice in a timely manner.

brief: Today's brief highlights the interconnectedness of global events, with tensions between the US and Iran threatening to escalate into a regional conflict, while the killing of Indian seafarers near Ukraine's Odesa port underscores the human cost of war on international trade. Meanwhile, India's economic growth trajectory remains a key focus, with the government releasing new monthly production indices to improve GDP forecasting and the RBI adopting a wait-and-see approach to monetary policy amidst conflicting global pressures. As India aims to become a developed economy by 2047, significant investments in energy security, maritime protection, and emerging technologies like AI will be crucial, with the country's net FDI inflows expected to rebound to $15 billion in FY27.

Geopolitics.title: Iran's Top Military Command Warns of Regional Escalation if U.S. Strikes Nuclear Sites
Geopolitics.summary: Iran's top military command has warned that a US attack on its nuclear sites would lead to a regional escalation, targeting US and allied interests. This statement highlights the increased tensions surrounding Iran's nuclear program and regional geopolitics. The warning suggests a potential expansion of conflict in the region.

Geopolitics.title: Odesa tragedy : Wars taking their toll on Indian seafarers
Geopolitics.summary: The killing of Indian seafarers on a merchant vessel hit by Russian cruise missiles near Ukraine's Odesa port has highlighted the dangers faced by civilian maritime workers amidst armed hostilities. The incident threatens the freedom of navigation that underpins the global economy. It also underscores the human cost of war on international trade and commerce.

Geopolitics.title: NSA Doval discusses enhancing energy security, protecting maritime corridors during Riyadh visit
Geopolitics.summary: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval discussed enhancing energy security and protecting maritime corridors during his visit to Riyadh. The talks aimed to review the progress in India-Saudi bilateral relations and address key security concerns. The discussion highlights the importance of energy security and maritime protection in the region.

Economy.title: India ran a $4-trillion economy half-blind for decades. That just ended
Economy.summary: The Indian government has started releasing monthly production indices for 19 service industries, which could improve GDP forecasting, monetary policy analysis, and investment research. This move aims to fill the gap in tracking the service sector, which accounts for more than half of the economy. The new indices will use GST returns and regulatory data to estimate changes in real output.

Economy.title: Can India become a developed economy by 2047? Why 9% growth, $200 billion FDI and AI matter
Economy.summary: To become a developed nation by 2047, India needs to sustain 9% annual economic growth, which requires increased investment, stronger manufacturing, and leadership in emerging technologies like AI. Former NITI Aayog CEO Amitabh Kant emphasizes the importance of policy stability and globally competitive companies in achieving this goal. India's growth trajectory will depend on its ability to attract significant foreign investment and leverage technology.

Economy.title: Energy shock vs AI boom: RBI should act as and when external shocks push domestic data in a direction, not until then
Economy.summary: The global economy is facing conflicting pressures from an energy shock and an AI-driven tech boom, which will impact India's monetary policy. The RBI is likely to adopt a wait-and-see approach, making policy decisions based on domestic data rather than reacting to external shocks immediately. This approach will help the RBI navigate the complexities of the global economy and make informed decisions.

Economy.title: Climate change is causing structural shifts in India’s inflation dynamics-forecasting must adapt
Economy.summary: Climate change is leading to more frequent supply shocks and weather disruptions, which are affecting India's inflation dynamics. As a result, the country's inflation targeting framework needs to incorporate climate-driven price instability risks to ensure effective monetary policy action. Other central banks are already taking steps to address this issue, and India must adapt its forecasting to account for these changes.

Economy.title: India's net FDI inflows likely to reach $15 bn in FY27
Economy.summary: India's net FDI inflows are expected to rebound to $15 billion in fiscal year 2027, driven by healthy gross inflows and moderating repatriation outflows. A nuanced FDI framework and policy measures are expected to boost foreign investment, while outbound FDI from India is also projected to remain healthy. This signals a positive outlook for India's capital account and economy.

Business.title: MS Dhoni’s latest startup bet isn’t gaming or fintech. It’s rooftop solar
Business.summary: MS Dhoni has invested in SolarSquare, a rooftop solar company, and will also be its brand ambassador. This investment is part of a $53 million funding round, which aims to boost India's clean energy growth. The move highlights the growing interest in renewable energy in India.

Business.title: India needs $22.7 trillion for net zero, faces $6.5-trillion funding gap: IEEFA
Business.summary: India requires a significant investment of $22.7 trillion by 2070 to achieve its net-zero targets. However, the country faces a substantial funding gap of $6.5 trillion, which needs to be addressed through strengthened climate-transition disclosures and business reporting. This will help secure the necessary capital and global funding.

Business.title: India's Banking Sector Eyes Sustainable Growth as Retail Lending and Capex Shape FY27 Outlook
Business.summary: India's banking sector is expected to experience sustainable growth in FY27, driven by strong asset quality, stable funding costs, and healthy credit growth. The sector's performance will depend on balanced retail lending, a revival in corporate capex, and prudent regulatory oversight. This will help maintain financial stability and support the sector's long-term growth.

Business.title: India Inc is returning to banks for money, but retail borrowers aren’t keeping pace
Business.summary: India's largest private banks are witnessing a shift in credit demand, with corporate and SME lending driving loan growth, while retail borrowing remains subdued. This shift is driven by stronger working capital demand, refinancing, and business banking, but slower consumer credit growth and weaker CASA ratios continue to impact banks' funding mix and margins.

Business.title: Emergent Raises Series C at $1.5 Billion Valuation, Becomes Unicorn in a Year of Launch
Business.summary: Emergent, an AI software creation platform, has raised $130 million in Series C funding, valuing the company at $1.5 billion and making it a unicorn within a year of its launch. The funding round was led by Creaegis and included participation from prominent investors such as Khosla Ventures and SoftBank Vision Fund 2. This investment will help Emergent further develop its platform and expand its operations.

National Affairs.title: Time can't cure illegality': SC orders demolition of illegal structures in UP's Meerut
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered the demolition of all illegal constructions in Meerut, including unauthorized additions in EWS houses and setback encroachments. This decision aims to curb illegal constructions and ensure adherence to the law. The order is significant as it sets a precedent for addressing similar issues across the country.

National Affairs.title: SC reluctant to take up PIL on paper leaks; asks what and how much can courts do
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has expressed reluctance to entertain a PIL seeking its intervention to curb question paper leaks in competitive exams. The court questioned the extent of its role in addressing this issue, highlighting the limitations of judicial intervention. This decision underscores the complexities of addressing paper leaks and the need for a multi-faceted approach.

National Affairs.title: SC asks Centre, CPCB to respond to allegations of non-utilisation of environmental relief fund
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has asked the Centre and the Central Pollution Control Board to respond to allegations of non-utilisation of the environmental relief fund, which has over Rs 1,000 crore. The fund, meant for road accident victims, has remained unused, prompting the court to seek an explanation. This case highlights the need for transparency and accountability in the use of environmental relief funds.

brief: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East continues to have far-reaching implications, with rising oil prices posing a significant risk to India's GDP growth in FY27, according to the International Monetary Fund. As the global economy navigates these challenges, India's economic growth prospects, including its potential to become a developed economy by 2047, will depend on its ability to sustain 9% annual growth and attract significant investment.

Despite a 4.5% drop in volumes, the import bill soared to Rs 4.64 lakh crore. This surge in oil prices has significant implications for India's economy and trade.

The company had argued that India did not provide sufficient investigation data during its probe. This development is significant as it is the largest tax issue for Pernod in India.

National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that a juvenile accused of a heinous crime, such as murder, can be tried as an adult. This decision is significant as it marks a shift in the approach to dealing with juvenile offenders who commit serious crimes. The court's ruling is aimed at ensuring that such offenders are held accountable for their actions.

National Affairs.summary: The government has informed Parliament that it is closely monitoring developments in its trade relationship with the US and remains engaged with Washington. Geopolitics.title: Balochistan has declared 'independence' from Pakistan. But can it really become a country?
Geopolitics.summary: Balochistan's declaration of independence from Pakistan raises questions about its potential statehood. The region's path to independence would depend on international law and global diplomacy, including recognition by other countries and eventual UN membership. The situation has implications for regional and global politics.

Economy.title: Crisil sees volatile FY27: Inflation to rise to 5.1%, growth to slow; RBI may hike rates
Economy.summary: According to a Crisil report, India's economy is expected to face challenges in 2026-27 due to rising inflation and slowing growth, despite easing geopolitical tensions and lower crude oil prices. The RBI may respond with cautious policy measures, including potential rate hikes. This could impact the country's overall economic outlook and growth trajectory.

Economy.title: Global economy tipping towards a sharp slowdown, India ‘may lose a step’ too: Moody's Analytics
Economy.summary: Moody's Analytics has warned that the global economy is headed for a slower growth phase over the next two years, with India also expected to lose momentum. This slowdown could be triggered by an extended US-Iran conflict, leading to a fresh energy shock, reignited inflation, and tighter monetary policy. India's economy may be impacted as a result, affecting its growth prospects.

Economy.title: Former RBI governor Raghuram Rajan, 2 other Indians appointed in key US Federal Reserve task forces
Economy.summary: Raghuram Rajan, former RBI governor, and two other Indians have been appointed to key US Federal Reserve task forces, which will review areas of US monetary policy. This move indicates the recognition of Indian expertise in global economic matters and may provide valuable insights for US monetary policy. The appointments could also enhance India's influence in global economic decision-making.

Economy.title: UK CETA, not quite the king's ransom expected from a landmark trade deal
Economy.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic Trade Agreement (CETA) may not bring the expected benefits, as small export businesses may struggle with British regulations. The agreement could also restrict India's policy-making abilities, affecting access to affordable medications and control over the digital economy. Despite these challenges, the deal may still offer new market opportunities for Indian exporters.

Business.title: AI IPO wave: Why India's start-up ecosystem could be an indirect winner
Business.summary: The upcoming IPOs of OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX may indirectly benefit India's startup ecosystem by reviving VC funding, improving exits, and boosting deep-tech investment. This could strengthen India's long-term AI ambitions and reshape global venture capital flows. Successful AI listings may also lead to increased investment in India's startup ecosystem.

Business.title: SBI Funds raises Rs 1880 crore in pre-IPO funding
Business.summary: SBI Funds has raised Rs 1880 crore in pre-IPO funding from investors including WhiteOak Capital and 3P India. The funding was raised through a private placement of equity shares at an issue price of Rs 574 per share. This investment will help SBI Funds ahead of its potential IPO.

Business.title: Aukera raises Rs 90 crore in fresh funding
Business.summary: Aukera, a premium lab-grown diamond jewellery brand, has raised Rs 90 crore in fresh funding from investors including Alteria Capital and InnoVen Capital. This funding round comes less than a year after the company raised $15 million in equity funding. The investment will help Aukera accelerate its national expansion plans.

Business.title: Bank credit growth to ease as corporates tap bond markets
Business.summary: Bank credit growth is expected to moderate in the upcoming fiscal year as large corporations increasingly use domestic bond markets and overseas borrowings for funding. This shift is driven by the Reserve Bank of India's policy measures, which make external funding more attractive for businesses. Overall credit demand remains healthy, but banks will finance a smaller share.

Business.title: Neuberg Diagnostics plans IPO in FY28
Business.summary: Neuberg Diagnostics plans to launch an initial public offering in fiscal year 2028, aiming to raise approximately Rs 3,500-4,000 crore. The company will also secure Rs 5000 crore in a pre-IPO funding round for acquisitions, targeting pan-India growth and new diagnostic technologies. Neuberg expects to become the second-largest player in the diagnostics market.

Business.title: Corporate Paper issuances touch 5-yr high at Rs 2.53 lakh crore
Business.summary: Indian companies raised Rs 2.53 lakh crore through commercial papers in June, a near five-year high. This surge was driven by attractive pricing, seasonal needs, and debt refinancing, with companies shifting from costlier bank loans to cheaper short-term market funding. The Reserve Bank of India's liquidity infusions also contributed to favorable conditions.

National Affairs.title: Pinarayi slams Kerala govt's stand in Waqf Board case, calls HC order 'shocking'
National Affairs.summary: Kerala Opposition leader Pinarayi Vijayan has criticized the state government's stance in a case related to the Waqf Board, accusing it of siding with petitioners and paralyzing the board. The issue revolves around the administration of religious institutions and the state government's role in it. The case has sparked debate about the secular character of India and the influence of the Sangh Parivar agenda.

National Affairs.title: SC allows release of animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' post 'Lord Jagannath Yatra'
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has permitted the all-India release of the animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' after the Lord Jagannath Yatra festivities. The film's release was allowed as it is based on a web series already available on YouTube. The court's decision comes after considering the film's content and its potential impact.

National Affairs.title: UP Government's Bulldozer Action Against Jauhar University Sparks Controversy
National Affairs.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government's decision to demolish Mohammad Ali Jauhar University has sparked controversy, with critics accusing it of communal politics and contempt of the Supreme Court. The university plans to challenge the demolition order in court, raising questions about the legal due process. The issue has been criticized by Bhim Army leader Chandra Shekhar Azad.

National Affairs.title: SC acts after courtroom chaos, tightens rules for litigants-in-person
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has tightened rules for litigants-in-person after a recent incident of courtroom chaos. The decision was made at a full court meeting of all Supreme Court judges and aims to maintain order and discipline in the court. The new rules will regulate the behavior of litigants who choose to represent themselves in court.

National Affairs.title: 1994 murder case: SC questions Bihar govt’s move to grant remission to former MP Anand Mohan
National Affairs.summary: The Supreme Court has questioned the Bihar government's decision to grant remission to former MP Anand Mohan, who was convicted of murder in 1994. The court observed that the decision seemed to be biased in favor of the convict, with all factors working in his favor to secure release. The case has raised concerns about the fairness of the remission process.
